title: VBS script file should be .vbs, not .vb
author: philgoetz
body:
README.md says,

> instead of a shortcut, create a .vb file (you can call it PersistentWindows as Administrator.vb) and add this to it:

That should say .vbs (visual basic script) file, not .vb (visual basic source) file.

title: There is no PersistentWindows.exe in PersistentWindows-master.zip
author: philgoetz
body: The instructions in README.md on installing PersistentWindows say to download PersistentWindows-master.zip, unzip it, and then, in one way or another, set up a task to run PersistentWindows.exe .  There is no PersistentWindows.exe in PersistentWindows-master.zip, and no instructions on how to create it.  Please explain how to create it.

title: Taskbar fails to restore properly 50% of the time
author: MFHFozzy
body:
I have a 3 monitor setup,  and the task bar is set to the right most monitor, on the right side (vertical task bar).
About 50% of the time, its not getting restored to the correct monitor, and instead stays on the center monitor.
I never see the cursor even go to the task bar to attempt moving it.

title: cmd.exe Windows seem to be getting closed on their own?
author: virtuallysurreal
body:
Windows 10, ESU Enrolled. All Current Updates.

Fairly customized system, which is why I have PersistentWindows 5.75 installed.

I was tired of things changing on their own when Windows was being stupid, changing the size of program and other windows, putting them off-screen, etc.

One thing I noticed after installing PersistentWindows was that my cmd.exe GUI windows used for performing tasks, checking things, etc., would be gone when I came back to them at a later time to resume work, checks, etc.

The biggest change since this started occurring was adding PersistentWindows, so I wanted to check if this was a known issue. Searched and could not find any mention of it anywhere.

Has anyone else come across this or had this happen to them?

Typical usage of cmd.exe is having one standard user window open for some commands, but also running one with admin rights for the commands requiring those, if that has something to do with the windows disappearing (or being closed).

I checked Windows Logs > Application and only could find PersistentWindows info on restoring cmd.exe after it's relaunched, unless I need to be more specific in the search.

Going to try to look into the task and see about exempting cmd.exe and look further into some of the other options, but this just happening like this "out of the box" was something that was a bit concerning, if it was caused by PersistentWindows, so definitely going to chase some things down a rabbit hole to see what I can find, but wanted to see if anyone else has experienced this or if it's something that may need addressing if it is caused by PersistentWindows to help others avoid problems. If anyone has specific suggestions, I'm open to that as well.

I had most things set default, but will be setting things up as standalone to put logs in a subfolder.
Seems I need to add the Z-order flag, as it's not default for automatic restore, so I'll be doing that.
I'll be disabling the webpage commander (browser bloat) that I don't need.

Anything I should be looking at, trying, testing, etc.?

[Trying to keep them open now to get a better timeframe of when they close to get better logs instead of a ton of useless junk]

title: Concatenate Syntax error inputed into batch start aux.ps1   (9,21):UserId:
author: sobepromotions
body:
auto_start_pw.bat run as admin fails on my PC, manual scheduling is the workaround but I think its just a small syntax error on line 9 colum 21 of the xml the ps1 creates.

It's rejecting my UserID environment variable computer name.  Using $env:USERNAME instead of $env:USERDOMAIN or $env:COMPUTERNAME, resulting in failure at line 25.  Throwing ObjectNotFound and NullArray errors at lines 27-38. 

Maybe I'm wrong also, I'm not a true wizard, I'm wrong often.

title: Issue with screenshot - not possible to select screenshot area because window moves. Permanently ignore?
author: GeeZuZz
body:
I ran into a problem where Windows 11's built in screenshot app (WIN+SHIFT+S) would move the area to a different monitor once I started selecting an area, making it impossible to take screenshots of areas.

Problem was solved with  -ignore_process "SnippingTool.exe". I suggest this be permanently ignored, since moving the window completely breaks its functionality.

title: CPU Performance
author: Hempflingclub
body:
The program uses ~8-100% of 1 CPU Core depending on doing nothing or a restore just happend.

Is there perhaps a way for the program to only save window positions every x seconds (120 would probably be fine), and otherwise only react on monitor reconnect events?

title: Restore Window position of minimized windows
author: Hempflingclub
body: On trying doesnt seem to restore minimized windows. Is there an option to do this? Is this feature planned?

title: App is not restoring on display wake
author: RyanThaDude
body:
Now that 5.75 has the white list, I decided to give PW a try again. I really have issue with Microsoft Excel restoring on my dual-monitor upon wake. So, I cleared all the old settings and old files, and started fresh. I inserted the parameters in the `auto_start_pw_aux.ps1` file, ran `auto_start_pw.bat` as administrator, and has properly setup Task Scheduler. Launched the app in Task Scheduler and PW appears in the tray as expected. I open a couple of Excel windows, position and size them. When the displays go to sleep and wake up, the Excel windows do not snap back into its moved position but stays on the primary monitor and the size is changed. This is the same behavior I was having when PW was not being used, so it seems PW is not doing something right, or I may have something set wrong...

Command line options: `-splash=0 -delay_auto_restore 2.5 -care_process "excel.exe"`

This is what appears in Event Viewer from PW (each line is its own event):
```
Display setting changing Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160
Display setting changed Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160
Restore aborted for Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160
no need to restore fresh session Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160
Display setting changing Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160__Display_Loc3840x0_Res2560x1440
Display setting changed Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160__Display_Loc3840x0_Res2560x1440
Restore finished in pass 4 with 0 windows recovered for display setting Display_Loc0x0_Res3840x2160__Display_Loc3840x0_Res2560x1440
```

For info, I run two different LG 27-inch monitors; primary at 3840x2160/60Hz and secondary at 2560x1440/144Hz, both connected via DisplayPort. Windows 11 Pro 25H2, RTX 5060Ti, 64 GB DDR4, Ryzen 9-3900x.

title: Only works on single desktop.
author: b-hayes
body:
Trying the app for the first time so if there is something I can do to fix it let me know.

**Steps to replicate**

- open some windows on desktop 1 and 2.
- on desktop 2 run a game that changes to a smaller resolution and then quit it.
- go back to desktop 1 and see all your windows are small boxes.

PS - On windows 10 no other window managers installed.

title: PersistentWindows 5.73 spontaneously hanging, with high CPU utilization
author: Sporking
body:
I have been noticing that PersistentWindows sometimes hangs, even when it was running correctly before. At first I thought it was because it would incorrectly restore when I switched monitors on/off (directly or via my KVM switch to switch them between computers) but today it happened twice when I was working without switching between monitors, so I now know that that's not necessary to exhibit the bug.

Symptoms:
1) PersistentWindows working normally. Then, suddenly...
2) Windows (for programs I start) don't get repositioned anymore on program startup.
3) Right-clicking the PersistentWindows icon in the Windows System Tray does nothing. Does not bring up a right-click menu as it usually does.
4) PersistentWindows shows up in the Task Manager list as "Power Usage: Very High". This seems similar to issue #432.
It may be relevant that I have had some Windows "Sticky Notes" app pages open on my screen. I have noticed in the past that PersistentWindows sometimes has problems restoring those onscreen and/or to the proper monitor. Perhaps because of the "always on top" behavior of the Sticky Notes app.

In my most recent instance, I was developing a Java GUI program. I noticed that when it appeared onscreen, it would get moved to the last location I had moved it to (despite code that I had written to position it in the middle of the screen when it started up). I eventually realized that PersistentWindows was the likely cause of this.

As I was performing some experiments attempting to figure out what to do about this (repeatedly stopping and restarting my program), I noticed that the repositioning suddenly stopped happening. I then went to PersistentWindows and right-clicked its icon in the Windows System Tray. I noticed that the usual right-click menu for PersistentWindows didn't appear as I expected. When I used Task Manager to kill and then Task Scheduler to restart PersistentWindows, I noticed that it showed as "Power usage: Very High". I killed the process and then restarted it via Task Scheduler, and then it started behaving normally again.

Note that I did not turn on or off any monitors between the time that PersistentWindows was behaving correctly and the time that I discovered that it had hung. All I did was restart my Java program several times.

A short time later (maybe 10 minutes) this happened again.

The reason that I knew to check for right-click behavior is because this problem has been happening to me spontaneously and intermittently for some time (not necessarily when I was writing Java programs) for quite some number of months. I would use my KVM to switch my 3 monitors over to a secondary computer, and then when I switched back, my windows would not be repositioned as I expected, but would instead be all piled up in one of my monitors. I eventually realized that when this happened, the PersistentWindows icon in the Windows System Tray would be unresponsive to a right-click, and restarting it would fix the problem (for a while).

I am not sure if this matters, but I have also experienced problems with windows appearing offscreen after a restore. I wonder if perhaps PersistentWindows tries to restore windows to an illegal location (or with an illegal size) and then waits for confirmation from Windows that they have been restored, which never comes because Windows has rejected the proposed location and size of one or more windows as illegal and is therefore no longer trying to restore the windows. (Note that the size of the window I that PersistentWindows was trying to restore my Java program to was actually larger than the screen: the top of the program was onscreen, but the bottom two corners were below the edge of the screen, potentially by quite a bit. Perhaps the fact that part of the restored window was offscreen is relevant to this bug.) Another possibility is that Windows itself had put the windows in a partially offscreen position, and PersistentWindows' attempt to move them onscreen caused problems (possibly because they didn't fit).

Between the first time this hang happened today and the second time, I enabled the Windows 11 features "Remember window locations based on monitor connection" and "Minimize windows when a monitor is disconnected". This change did not seem to prevent PersistentWindows from hanging.

Overall, this looks a lot like issue #432, which is currently marked as "closed" due to lack of reproducibility. It seems to me that this issue should probably not have been closed. I haven't looked at PersistentWindows's source code yet, but it seems to me as though this issue would possibly caused by some kind of a "spin loop" where PersistentWindows is waiting for an event from Windows that will never happen (such as notification that a window has been positioned to where PersistentWindows asked for it to be positioned?), causing the PersistentWindows GUI thread to be hung in a loop waiting for that event and therefore unable to process other events like the right-click menu handling.

(Also note that in general it is problematic to do tasks like waiting for events on the GUI thread, for exactly this reason: if the event takes a long time, the GUI thread becomes unresponsive: this should generally be done on a background thread, with progress reported by shared data (with mutex locking as appropriate) which the GUI thread can check occasionally to check for progress while still being able to process other GUI-related events (like mouse right-click handling, etc.) in the meantime.)

Please look into this ASAP! Every loop in PersistentWindows should have some kind of a timeout or backup plan for situations when Windows doesn't return the expected response (or doesn't do it quickly enough). It should be impossible for responses from the OS to cause PersistentWindows to enter into a state where its GUI handling thread is fully occupied for an indefinite amount of time and therefore unable to process events like mouse-click events. Also, if a single window doesn't get restored for whatever reason, that shouldn't prevent other windows from being restored.

Thanks, and please let me know if there is some way I can help (by running a specially-annotated version with debug output to figure out where the hang is, for instance.)

title: Latest build behaves wrong with shortcuts disabled
author: Roccoderra
body:
Hi there, ever since I have upgraded to the latest build, in 3ds max (where I use the alt key a lot) my floating windows change sizes form time to time.
I have these in the task scheduler actions since the beginning of using this program: "-delay_start 5 -splash=0 -hotkey_window=0 -delay_auto_capture 1.0 -auto_restore_missing_windows=1"
Usually when things start falling apart I just use restore windows from disk (i made months ago)
Up until latest everything was ok.

Version: 5.72.9491.21925

Should I revert back to previous version?

title: Add install/uninstaller
author: andrisi
body: Great app, but while making making windows spiffy simple, it's installation is like a barrier. Catch 21? Please make an installer. Thanks a lot!

title: Is it possible to not resize windows
author: Newgap
body:
I would like windows to be restored to their monitor of origin, but often they are resize without reason and sometime to ridicule dimensions.
Also, maximized window should stay maximized on the new monitor.
Could it be possible to add an option to disable window resize if it does not already exists?

title: Very high power consumption
author: Zebra447
body: How is this app using 5% of my 16-core 9950X sitting idle in the systray doing nothing? It's using as much CPU as running a VM...

title: Second monitor doesn't restore windows + taskbar icons are stuck on main monitor
author: Zebra447
body:
Two problems, but first here's my setup: I use a HDMI extender over ethernet on my second monitor, but it has some issues where I lose signal when monitor is sleeping, and I have to unplug and replug the extender. To fix it, I added a smart plug that disconnects the extender and reconnect it during the night.

Persistent Windows worked fine initially, but now I wake up every day and all the windows from my second monitor were moved to my main monitor.

The second problem is the same as another user described here:
https://github.com/kangyu-california/PersistentWindows/issues/429

I have 4 monitors, but only the #2 is getting reset with the smart plug. Yet, every morning, all the taskbar icons from monitors 2-3-4 are moved to the main monitor. The windows in monitor 3-4 are still in the correct position, but the taskbar icons are showing BOTH on the main and second displays. The only way to fix this issue is to take the window from second displays, move them to the main display, then move back to second displays, and then the taskbar icon is removed from the main monitor.

I also use DisplayFusion btw.

title: Capture windows using command line option?
author: casper
body:
Hi. Great little program. 

There is a command-line option to restore a capture, but is there also a command-line option to **save** a capture?

I would like to automate saving and restoring at specific times using Windows Task Scheduler, but currently I can only restore using this method. 

I couldn't figure out how to also save window positions with command-line.
Is it possible to do somehow?

Thank you.

title: Doesn't move the taskbar icon
author: Bendzsike
body:
When waking up my monitors (primary: 2k, portrait: 1080p, both connected with DP) Persistent Windows will move Discord back to the portrait monitor, but the taskbar icon will stay on primary monitor until I manually move Discord to primary, then back to the portrait monitor.

System info:
OS: Windows 11 Home
Version: 24H2
OS build: 26100.6899

I installed it by running the .bat file as admin, didn't change any launch arguments.
Idk what else info would be relevant for this issue.
It can't be seen on the image, but the portrait monitor's taskbar doesn't contain the discord icon.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/853b6a0a-b2a9-4106-ba6e-72c76c71b3f3)
